title: Packera millelobata
intro: Packera millelobata, also known as Golden Ragwort, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, commonly found in eastern North America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Packera millelobata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into salves and creams for topical applications.
